description: Public Python-to-JavaScript SDK parity contract for the @traigent/sdk 0.2.0 parity release.

python.note: This manifest intentionally tracks Python develop, not the currently published Python package. JS 0.2.0 is forward-bound to the next Python 0.12.0 public surface unless Python 0.12.0 ships first.

conditionalRootExports.condition: Python runtime extends traigent.__all__ only when importing traigent.cloud.agent_dtos through the traigent.cloud package succeeds.
Python runtime extends traigent.__all__ only when importing traigent.cloud.dtos through the traigent.cloud package succeeds.

javascript.observedShaPolicy: Informational only. JS is validated as any build that satisfies requiredRootExports; observedSha is recorded for parity-drift postmortems.

releasePolicy.js020PythonReleaseOrder: JS 0.2.0 may ship before Python 0.12.0. If it does, release notes must describe this as forward-compatible with the next Python public surface, not parity with the currently published Python package.
releasePolicy.publishedPythonCompatibilityNote: Python 0.11.4 at pin time does not expose BenchmarkClient, BenchmarkClientConfig, Dataset, or EvaluationExample. Those symbols are included because the manifest tracks Python develop at 4fe3cd37.
refreshCadence: Refresh the manifest on every Python public SDK release candidate or release branch.
Refresh the manifest in the same PR family as any Python change to traigent.__all__.
Refresh the manifest before every JS minor parity release.
If neither SDK releases for a quarter, run a quarterly drift check and open a no-op or update PR documenting the result.

classificationDefinitions.matched: JS exports a production implementation or schema-backed type with the same public intent.
classificationDefinitions.additive-js-alias: JS exports a compatibility alias over an existing camelCase JS type or helper.
classificationDefinitions.deferred-schema: Python exposes the symbol, but JS cannot implement production behavior until TraigentSchema has the contract; JS may expose a throwing migration stub.
classificationDefinitions.deferred-backlog: Tracked for future JS production behavior or an open decision ticket; may be exported only as a throwing migration stub when also listed in stubRootExports.
classificationDefinitions.out-of-scope-permanent: Python-only runtime, analysis, plotting, validation, or internal surface that is not part of the JS SDK parity contract.

decisions.manifestLocation: TraigentSchema/parity/python-js-sdk.json is authoritative; JS and Python CI read this file instead of maintaining repo-local ignore lists.
decisions.pythonPinning: Only the Python SHA is pinned. JS is validated as any build that satisfies the manifest-required exports.
decisions.js020PythonReleaseOrder: JS 0.2.0 is allowed to ship before Python 0.12.0 as a forward-compatible parity release, provided release notes state that the manifest tracks next Python, not published Python 0.11.4.
decisions.manifestRefresh: TraigentSchema owns manifest refreshes for Python release candidates, Python __all__ changes, JS minor parity releases, and quarterly drift checks when release cadence is slow.
decisions.benchmarkClient: JS 0.2.0 no longer exports a BenchmarkClient stub. Python develop still root-exports BenchmarkClient/BenchmarkClientConfig, so they remain classified deferred-schema (no JS twin) until the benchmark dataset generation endpoint is schema-owned; they are intentionally absent from requiredRootExports and stubRootExports.
decisions.enterpriseAdminClient: JS 0.2.0 no longer exports an EnterpriseAdminClient migration stub. Python develop still root-exports EnterpriseAdminClient/EnterpriseAdminConfig, so they remain classified deferred-backlog (tracked in Traigent/traigent-js#30) but are intentionally absent from requiredRootExports and stubRootExports.
decisions.observabilityWrappers: Default-client helpers and the observe wrapper are deferred to 0.3.0 to avoid expanding the 0.2.0 parity release with new runtime semantics; track in Traigent/traigent-js#38.
decisions.addAgentSpan: Python root-exports add_agent_span (traigent.observability) on both develop and main; it is an observability-wrapper helper with no @traigent/sdk twin yet, so it is classified deferred-backlog alongside observe/get_default_observability_client and tracked with the other observability wrappers in Traigent/traigent-js#38 (resolves the js-public-parity manifest gap, TraigentSchema#117).
decisions.textDocument: Python root-exports TextDocument (traigent.api.parameter_ranges) — a trainable single-text-document parameter type for skill training (Choices[str], trainable=True). It is a concrete tunable type with no @traigent/sdk twin yet, so it is classified deferred-backlog pending a JS skill-training surface (TraigentSchema#117).
decisions.pinRefresh: Manifest Python pin advanced from c79bc47 to 4fe3cd37 (origin/develop) to cover the two root exports added since the previous pin — add_agent_span and TextDocument — that the live js-public-parity gate flagged (TraigentSchema#117).
decisions.advisorySelectionHelpers: Python exposes advisory strategy-preset selection and recommendation-catalog helpers in the 0.12.0 release line. JS 0.2.0 defers root exports until the JS advisory-helper surface is designed; these helpers are not schema DTOs or required JS clients.
decisions.frameworkDiscovery: Future implicit framework discovery must be opt-in, avoid global patching, log activation, and be deterministic across import order.

schemaRefresh.note: Auto-stamped by scripts/refresh_parity.py. Do not hand-edit this block.
